subject: UX & Research
title: The Original iPhone Usability
description: One-minute skill: The Original iPhone Usability.

hook.text: When Apple tested the iPhone internally, users couldn't unlock it. The fix: 'Slide to unlock.'

buildup.text: Apple's team tested relentlessly before the 2007 launch. Every gesture and animation was refined.

discovery.text: Pinch-to-zoom emerged from testing. Users naturally tried it with multi-touch. The gesture felt intuitive.

twist.text: Apple skipped testing the keyboard. Reviewers panicked. Users adapted faster than critics predicted.

climax.text: Apple's philosophy: test the interaction, not the concept. They didn't ask 'Want a touchscreen?' They tested feel.

punchline.text: Test how it feels, not whether people want it. Apple proved this.

quiz.question: What was Apple's testing philosophy for the original iPhone?
options: Test the interaction and feel, not the concept — they tested how touch worked, not if users wanted it
Test with focus groups to validate the concept first
Only test with Apple employees to maintain secrecy
